title: Chilli, Thyme & Parmesan Roast Fennel Recipe
summary: Roasted fennel is an elite side, its aniseed liquorice flavour mellows and it becomes super sweet. Try it alongside a steak with chimichurri or with a roasted shoulder of lamb or chicken. You're going to love this roast fennel recipe, Mob.

method.description: Heat your oven to 180°C. Line a large roasting tray with baking parchment.
Pick any of the green fennel fronds off the fennel and save them to top the roasted fennel later, then cut the roots off each fennel bulb. Cut in half lengthwise then cut each half into 3cm wedges.

Strip the thyme from the sprigs into a large bowl. Zest in the lemon then stir in the chilli flakes and a splash of olive oil. Add the fennel wedges, along with some salt and plenty of black pepper, toss so that the wedges get evenly coated.

Lay the fennel wedges onto the lined tray in an even layer. Roast for 30 mins, turning half way through, until soft and beginning to caramelise. When the fennel is nice and jammy, finely grate over the vegetarian hard cheese. Return to the oven for 10-15 mins until the cheese has melted and the fennel is completely tender.
Transfer the fennel to a serving bowl. Squeeze over the lemon juice and drizzle with a little more olive oil and scatter over the fennel fronds, to serve.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
